blocks.content: Cassandra looked up. Her mother was watching her with the particular attention she had — the kind that didn't announce itself, that had been paying attention quietly for several minutes before it spoke.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: Lighter. Or — resolved. Something like that.
Her father looked at her. He had put down his fork. He had his listening expression — the one she'd grown up with, the one that communicated: I'm here, I'm not going anywhere, take your time.
Maya had gone still beside her.
Cassandra looked at her pasta. Then she set down her fork too.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Can I — can I tell you all something?
She said it to the table more than to any one of them.
The room shifted its quality slightly, the way rooms do when something real is about to be said in them.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I've been spending a lot of time thinking about — about Ethan. About what he is to me. And about what's fair to him.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I talked to Lena today. And I think I've been — I've been protecting something. And I think the protection has started to cost more than what it's protecting.
Her mother set down her fork, very quietly.
